heaviest_rainfall.status: On 09 November 2025, a record rainfall of 47.89mm was measured, indicating an exceptionally wet day.  24.6mm per hour was recorded as falling at times during the 144.8 hour storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The rain rate reached 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, and resulted in 21.19mm of rain over 15.0 hours.  This is the highest rate recorded by our weather station.

longest_rain.status: The record duration for continuous rain was set on 09 November 2025, the storm lasted 144.8 hours with a total precipitation of 47.89mm and it reached 24.6mm per hour at times.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning strikes in one day since 11 April 2025 is 23 and it happened on 07 September 2025.

storm.status: The last storm was on 17 April 2026 which lasted for 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is currently no cold snap.  This year there have been 3 cold snaps, usually there are 3.7 cold snaps per year.

extreme.status: The maximum temperature difference seen is 26.7°C, observed on 29 April 2025 when the temperature fell to 0.4°C but also managed to reach 27.1°C on the same day.

heatwaves.status: At the moment, it is not a heatwaves.  This year has seen 0 heatwavess; typically, 1.9 heatwavess happen every year.

tropical.status: The previous tropical night was on 18 July 2025, with only 62 in total since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (211°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The highest wind gust ever seen is 128.2 mph on 27 January 2026.

run.status: To date, the maximum wind run recorded is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The record wind speed remains at 93.7 mph from 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 8.6°C), outside temperature (down 8.4°C), and inside temperature (down 2.0°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 14%).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in light levels (up 14981 lux) and cloud levels (up 1311 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in dew point temperature (up 9.3°C), light levels (up 11805 lux), outside temperature (up 10.5°C), and cloud levels (up 634 m) when compared to today.

Today’s average cloud levels is 1310.8m lower than yesterday, 372.0m lower than the past week, 634.4m lower than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 1100m to a maximum of 4850m, averaging 1484.3m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 1856.4m and 2120m respectively.

summary.summary: Inside; a mild and comfortable temperature is anticipated. Outside; icy, freezing temperatures. Also, the atmosphere will feel parched and dry and very little to no wind will be present. Chance of light rain, the sky may alternate between sunny and overcast, and weak sunlight because of clouds. No significant shifts in the weather are expected.

washing.phrase: The current temperature is too low (6.2°C) for optimal drying conditions.  Any drying will take longer.
